description: Assessment & Adaptation Pipeline — wire auto-level-up, unify CEFR progress schema, aggregate EVAL dimension scores, periodic assessments, mastery threshold consistency, dynamic mid-conversation scaffolding, vocabulary frequency enforcement, grammar weakness quests, C1/C2 CEFR support, gradual UI immersion, and automated tests

userStories.title: Wire auto-level-up with UI celebration
userStories.description: As a player, I need checkCEFRAdvancement() to actually be called after conversations so that my CEFR level advances when I meet thresholds instead of being stuck at A1 forever.
acceptanceCriteria: After calculateFluencyGain() runs in progress.ts, call checkCEFRAdvancement() with a CEFRProgressSnapshot of current wordsLearned, wordsMastered, conversationsCompleted, textsRead, grammarPatternsRecognized
If shouldAdvance=true, update cefrLevel in LanguageProgress and persist via data source
New level propagates to getNPCLanguageBehavior(), getHintBehavior(), filterQuestsByCEFR(), getCEFRTextComplexity()
Show a CEFR level-up celebration modal/toast with level description from cefr-mapping.ts and summary of what changes
Wire level-up into BabylonGame.ts after conversation end handler
Assert player_cefr_level() Prolog fact on advancement (matching AssessmentEngine._assertAssessmentResult pattern)
Safeguards: no demotion via auto-level-up, minimum time/conversation count since last advancement to prevent oscillation
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: checkCEFRAdvancement() in cefr-adaptation.ts line 267 is never called. Thresholds: A1→A2: 50 words + 3 convos, A2→B1: 150 words + 10 convos + 5 texts, B1→B2: 300 words + 25 convos + 15 texts.

userStories.title: Unify progress schema with cefrLevel column
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cefrLevel directly on the playerProgress table so that server-side queries can filter by CEFR level without parsing nested save data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add cefrLevel column to playerProgress in shared/schema.ts with text type, enum validation for A1/A2/B1/B2/C1/C2, default null
Create MongoDB migration adding the field to the Mongoose schema in mongo-storage.ts
On assessment completion (AssessmentEngine.onCompleted), write cefrLevel to both LanguageProgress and playerProgress
On auto-level-up, update playerProgress.cefrLevel alongside LanguageProgress.cefrLevel
Server API endpoint returns cefrLevel without loading full LanguageProgress blob
Server-side quest generation reads from playerProgress.cefrLevel
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: playerProgress table at shared/schema.ts line 1364 has level, experience, gold, stats but NO cefrLevel.

userStories.title: Aggregate EVAL dimension scores into long-term tracking
userStories.description: As a player, I need my NPC EVAL dimension scores (Vocabulary, Grammar, Fluency, Comprehension, Task Completion) accumulated over time so that the system can identify my weak areas and adapt.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse EVAL blocks (search for **EVAL** and END_EVAL patterns) extracting { vocabulary, grammar, fluency, comprehension, taskCompletion } scores 1-5
Add dimensionScores history array to LanguageProgress interface with timestamp, conversationId, npcId, scores
After each conversation with EVAL block, push parsed scores to history array
Implement computeAverageDimensionScores(lastN?) returning rolling averages across 5 dimensions
Implement computeDimensionTrend(dimension, windowSize) returning improving/stable/declining
Include recent dimension averages in periodic assessment context
Feed dimension scores into conversation-quality-scoring.ts as additional signal
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: EVAL blocks referenced in BabylonChatPanel.ts ~line 1762 and progress.ts line 232. Similar to parseGrammarFeedbackBlock() at progress.ts line 253.

userStories.title: Wire periodic assessment triggers at quest milestones
userStories.description: As a player, I need periodic assessments to actually trigger at quest completion milestones so that my CEFR level stays calibrated as I progress.
acceptanceCriteria: After each quest completion event on GameEventBus, call isPeriodicAssessmentLevel() from periodic-encounter.ts with completed quest count
Respect 60-minute cooldown (PERIODIC_ASSESSMENT_COOLDOWN_MS) between assessments
Create launchPeriodicAssessment() in BabylonGame.ts: pause gameplay, open AssessmentModalUI, run conversation phase (25 points, 5 dimensions), score result
Handle results: if score suggests higher/lower CEFR, trigger auto-level-up or flag for confirmation
Show pre-assessment notification with Accept/Postpone options; if postponed, retry after next quest
Quest journal shows when next periodic assessment is due
Periodic assessments update dimension scores in aggregated tracking system
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: periodic-encounter.ts line 52 has triggers at milestones [5, 10, 15, 20] and 60-min cooldown, but integration with game events is MISSING.

userStories.title: Fix mastery threshold inconsistency
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single canonical mastery definition so that vocabulary mastery is consistent across all systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all mastery threshold definitions: progress.ts calculateMasteryLevel(), cefr-adaptation.ts isWordMastered(), and any others
Create shared MASTERY_THRESHOLDS constant in shared/language/vocabulary-constants.ts with NEW/LEARNING/FAMILIAR/MASTERED levels
Update cefr-adaptation.ts isWordMastered() to use canonical thresholds (currently 5 encounters + 1 use — too low)
Update progress.ts calculateMasteryLevel() to import from shared constants
All UI components displaying mastery use same thresholds
Unit test verifies all mastery checks in the codebase agree on thresholds
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: progress.ts: mastered at 8+ correct uses. cefr-adaptation.ts: mastered at 5 encounters + 1 use. These disagree.

userStories.title: Dynamic mid-conversation scaffolding
userStories.description: As a player, I need NPCs to detect when I'm struggling mid-conversation and simplify their speech in real-time so that I don't get stuck.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ConversationDifficultyMonitor class at shared/game-engine/logic/ConversationDifficultyMonitor.ts
Track struggle metrics per turn: grammar error rate (>50% corrected), target language usage (<20% for 3 turns), response length (1-2 words for 3+ turns), conversation quality score
Maintain weighted struggle score (0-1); emit difficulty_adjustment_needed when >0.6 for 2+ consecutive turns
When scaffolding triggers, modify NPC system prompt: reduce target language by 20%, shorten sentences, add inline translations, switch to simplified mode
Show subtle visual indicator when scaffolding activates
Track scaffolding activations in dimension scores for potential over-promotion detection
Add reverse: stretch challenge when player performs exceptionally well
Scaffolding state resets between conversations
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: NPC language mode is locked at conversation start via assignNPCLanguageMode() and never re-evaluated.

userStories.title: Mid-conversation NPC language mode re-evaluation
userStories.description: As a player, I need NPCs to update their language mode when my CEFR level advances between conversations so that returning to the same NPC reflects my improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify getNPCLanguageBehavior() is called with current cefrLevel at each conversation start (not cached from session start)
If language mode is cached anywhere in BabylonGame.ts, add cache invalidation on CEFR level change
NPC comments on player improvement when CEFR advances between conversations
Metadata request in BabylonChatPanel.ts passes current (not stale) CEFR level each time
Full flow works: player at A1 talks to NPC (80-90% English), advances to A2, same NPC now uses 50-70% English
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: assignNPCLanguageMode() uses deterministic hash of NPC ID + CEFR distributions. Mode should naturally update if called with new cefrLevel.

userStories.title: Enforce vocabulary frequency ranges in LLM prompts
userStories.description: As a player, I need NPC speech to actually use vocabulary within my CEFR frequency range so that I'm exposed to words I can learn, not arbitrary vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: Augment buildLanguageModeDirective() with frequency guidance per level: A1 top 200 words, A2 top 500, B1 top 1500, B2 unrestricted
Create/import word frequency list stored at data/seed/language/word-frequency-ranks.json
Add post-generation validation: check target-language words against frequency list and flag out-of-range words for inline translation
Include vocabulary range in conversation metadata sent to server
Update getCEFRTextComplexity() to include frequency range in text complexity parameters
Apply to all LLM content generation: NPC conversations, quest text, assessment content, reading materials
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: cefrToVocabularyRange() at line 302 maps levels to frequency ranks but is never enforced in LLM prompts.

userStories.title: Generate error-correction quests from weak grammar patterns
userStories.description: As a player, I need targeted quests generated from my weakest grammar patterns so that I can practice and improve specific areas I struggle with.
acceptanceCriteria: Create grammar weakness analyzer at shared/language/grammar-weakness-analyzer.ts: compute error rate per GrammarPattern, identify patterns with >50% error rate and 3+ attempts

Create grammar quest generator at shared/quests/grammar-quest-generator.ts: Grammar Drill (conversation with teacher NPC), Correction Challenge (read passage with intentional errors), Writing Practice (write sentences scored by LLM)
After conversations with grammar feedback, check weakness threshold and generate quest if none active for that pattern
Include weak grammar patterns in NPC system prompts for natural modeling of correct usage
Track quest effectiveness: monitor error rate improvement after completion
Integrate with periodic assessment to include weak-pattern questions
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: parseGrammarFeedbackBlock() at progress.ts line 253 tracks correctCount/incorrectCount per pattern but nothing analyzes or acts on weaknesses.

userStories.title: Add C1/C2 CEFR support
userStories.description: As an advanced player, I need C1 and C2 CEFR levels to exist so that I have continued progression beyond B2.
acceptanceCriteria: Add C1 (85%) and C2 (95%) score thresholds to CEFR_THRESHOLDS in cefr-mapping.ts; update mapScoreToCEFR()
Add advancement thresholds: B2→C1 (500 words, 50 convos, 30 texts, 20 grammar patterns), C1→C2 (800 words, 100 convos, 50 texts, 40 grammar patterns)
Add C1/C2 to CEFR_NPC_DISTRIBUTIONS: C1 100% natural with sophisticated vocabulary, C2 native-speaker level
Extend getNPCLanguageBehavior() and buildLanguageModeDirective() with C1/C2 directives
Extend getHintBehavior(): C1 no translation/hints, C2 optional monolingual dictionary
Add C1/C2 exam content to arrival-encounter.ts (literary/academic texts, argumentation prompts)
Extend getCEFRTextComplexity() and cefrToVocabularyRange() for C1/C2
Update filterQuestsByCEFR() and CEFRLevel type definition everywhere
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Entire system caps at B2 currently.

userStories.title: Gradual UI immersion at B2+
userStories.description: As an advanced player, I need game interface elements to transition to the target language at higher CEFR levels so that I get full immersion.
acceptanceCriteria: Create UI localization system at shared/language/ui-localization.ts with getUIString(key, immersionLevel) and immersion percentage by CEFR
Immersion levels: A1-A2=0%, B1=10% (location names), B2=30% (action prompts, inventory, menus), C1=60% (quest descriptions, notifications), C2=90% (nearly everything except system messages)
Implement hover-to-reveal English for translated UI elements
Progressive rollout: don't switch all elements at once within each level
Smooth transition when CEFR level changes: phase in over minutes of gameplay
Settings toggle: UI Language Immersion: Auto / English Only / Maximum
Start with action prompts and building labels as minimum viable implementation
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: ALL UI is currently in English regardless of CEFR level.

userStories.title: Automated tests for assessment-adaptation pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test suite covering the assessment-to-adaptation pipeline so that regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: shared/assessment/__tests__/cefr-advancement.test.ts: test checkCEFRAdvancement() at/below/above thresholds, max level behavior
shared/language/__tests__/mastery-consistency.test.ts: import mastery definitions from all locations, assert agreement
shared/language/__tests__/cefr-adaptation.test.ts: test getNPCLanguageBehavior(), getHintBehavior(), filterQuestsByCEFR(), cefrToVocabularyRange() per level
shared/game-engine/__tests__/ConversationDifficultyMonitor.test.ts: struggle detection, scaffolding activation, reset between conversations
shared/assessment/__tests__/periodic-assessment-trigger.test.ts: milestone levels, cooldown, CEFR level update
Integration test simulating full journey: initial assessment → A1 → conversations → auto-level-up → periodic assessment → grammar weakness → quest → improvement
All tests pass
